headings.usage: Use for primary titles and large data numbers.

body.usage: Use for all standard body text and descriptions.

mono.usage: Used for live AI verdicts, node tags, probability scores, and 'analysis' annotations.

layout.spacing: Generous. Use p-6, p-8, and p-12 for dashboard panels to let data breathe.
layout.grid_strategy: Mode B (Control Room Grid). A fixed right sidebar (w-80 or w-96 on lg screens) for the live 'verdict' and synthetic score. Main content area holds the full-screen dynamic network graph.
layout.borders: 1px solid border-black/5 for soft structure without heavy lines.
layout.radius: rounded-2xl for main cards, rounded-full for badges and small UI.

surfaces.cards: White background, delicate 1px border (#00000010), ambient soft shadow (shadow-sm). No heavy drop shadows.
surfaces.glass_panels: Crystal Glass style for scrolling annotations and popups. backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/70, 1px white border.
surfaces.graph_nodes: Circle nodes. Soft pastel fills based on category (e.g., pink for AI, mint for full-stack), dark charcoal text.

network_graph.instructions: Full height/width of the main section. Nodes represent projects, experiences, skills. Edges show relationships. Hover expands node into a detailed glass-morphic card overlay.

live_verdict_sidebar.structure: Sticky right sidebar. Contains 'Synthetic Self Score', progress/probability bars, and a typewritten analytical paragraph.
live_verdict_sidebar.visuals: Use IBM Plex Mono for data labels. Progress bars should have pastel fills and rounded edges.

scrolling_annotations.behavior: As user scrolls, fixed or absolutely positioned text elements fade in (e.g., 'DevOps cluster detected'). Use framer-motion for smooth entrance.

project_cards.interaction: Triggered on node click/hover. Expands into a rich detail card.
project_cards.content: Problem identified, why chosen, role, tech stack (tags), challenges, outcomes. Use structured grid inside.

motion_and_interactions.global: Micro-animations on hover (scale 1.02, subtle shadow increase). Custom cursor that acts as an 'analyzer reticle' (optional but encouraged).
motion_and_interactions.graph: Smooth physics on load. Nodes gently pulse.
motion_and_interactions.text: Staggered entrance animations. Live text generation effect for the Claude Sonnet verdicts.

textures.description: Soft pink and blue abstract texture
textures.usage: Use as a very low opacity (5-10%) background overlay for the main dashboard to add grain and depth.
Background for specific expanded project cards or hover states.

profile_images.description: Professional woman coding at office desk
profile_images.usage: Avatar or profile header in the verdict sidebar.

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure dark charcoal text on pastel backgrounds passes APCA. Never use white text on pastel backgrounds.
accessibility_and_testing.testing: All interactive elements, nodes, and buttons MUST have `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `data-testid="project-node-echotrace"`, `data-testid="verdict-sidebar"`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Install `react-force-graph-2d` for the central network graph.
Install `framer-motion` for scroll annotations and smooth expansions.
Use Shadcn UI for progress bars, badges, and cards, but severely override defaults to match the pastel, off-white aesthetic with 1px delicate borders.
Create a hybrid layout: a massive main canvas for the graph and a fixed right sidebar (`w-80` to `w-96` depending on breakpoint).
Simulate the 'live AI analysis' by having typewriter text effects in the sidebar when interacting with different graph nodes.
Do NOT use a dark cyberpunk theme. The entire UI must feel like a sophisticated, high-end editorial magazine applied to complex data analytics.
